name: TSCA Dataset
description: Temporal Stochastic Cellular Automata (TSCA) dataset for dual-rule identification and tau estimation. Each sample evolves under two ECA rules (f and g) that alternate row-by-row: at each timestep, the entire row is updated by rule f with probability tau or rule g with probability (1-tau). Features orbit data, pre-computed frac_stats, and per-step rule labels. Training data is generated on-the-fly; only the test split is stored on disk.
version_history: v9 (current); backward compatible with temporal_v5 — identical output with same SEED=42 split and N_ORBITS=8. Can symlink TSCA_Data from v5 instead of regenerating.

generation.update_scheme: Row-level temporal switching: at each timestep the entire grid row is updated by rule f with probability tau or rule g with probability (1-tau). One binary draw per timestep (harder than SCA's per-cell draws).

wolfram_classes.A: Class I — fixed point / uniform (rules: 0,8,32,40,128,136,160,168 and equivalents)

pairs.description: Each sample is a (rule_f, rule_g, tau) triple. Pairs are sampled to ensure coverage of all Wolfram class combinations (AxA, AxB, AxC, BxB, BxC, CxC).

field_descriptions.orbits: K independent orbit realizations per sample. Each orbit is [T, W] binary grid evolved under the temporal rule-switching process.
field_descriptions.frac_stats: 24 pre-computed fractional statistics across the K orbits (see compute_frac_stats in MODEL_TSCA.py). Encodes per-pattern activation frequencies and variance.
field_descriptions.rule_f_bits: 8-bit binary representation of rule f (LSB first). Stored as float32 for direct model input.
field_descriptions.rule_g_bits: 8-bit binary representation of rule g (LSB first). Stored as float32 for direct model input.
field_descriptions.taus: True tau value used during simulation. Probability that rule f is applied at each timestep (row-level draw).
field_descriptions.rule_f_ids: Integer rule number (0-255) for rule f.
field_descriptions.rule_g_ids: Integer rule number (0-255) for rule g.
field_descriptions.step_labels: Per-timestep binary label indicating which rule was applied at each step in each orbit. 0 = rule g, 1 = rule f. Shape [N, K, T-1] (first step has no label).

TSCA_Data/.train_pairs.npy: Array of shape [500, 2] — (rule_f, rule_g) training pairs
TSCA_Data/.test_pairs.npy: Array of shape [500, 2] — (rule_f, rule_g) test pairs

usage.symlink_note: If TSCA_Data already exists from temporal_v5 (same SEED=42, N_ORBITS=8), you can symlink instead: ln -s <path>/temporal_v5/TSCA_Data <path>/temporal_v9/TSCA_Data

loading_example.python:
import numpy as np
orbits      = np.load('TSCA_Data/test/orbits.npy')
frac        = np.load('TSCA_Data/test/frac_stats.npy')
taus        = np.load('TSCA_Data/test/taus.npy')
step_labels = np.load('TSCA_Data/test/step_labels.npy')
rule_f      = np.load('TSCA_Data/test/rule_f_ids.npy')
rule_g      = np.load('TSCA_Data/test/rule_g_ids.npy')

task.type: multi-output: dual classification + regression + sequence labeling

targets.rule_f: 8-bit rule bits or integer ID for rule f
targets.rule_g: 8-bit rule bits or integer ID for rule g
targets.step_labels: per-timestep binary sequence indicating which rule was applied [N_ORBITS, T-1]

ceiling_analysis.description: Information-theoretic signal analysis on the test set.
ceiling_analysis.signal_proxy: max(frac*(1-frac)) across 8 frac_stat channels (indices 8-15) — approximates tau*(1-tau) at disagreement patterns

categories.no_signal: max_var < 0.01 — rules agree on all patterns; tau and both rules are unrecoverable
categories.strong_signal: max_var >= 0.05 — solvable with a good model

ceiling_analysis.notes: TSCA is harder than SCA: only one binary draw per timestep (vs W independent draws in SCA), so frac estimates converge more slowly and tau symmetry (tau vs 1-tau) is harder to resolve.

comparison_notes.vs_SCA: Same rule split (SEED=42), same pair sampling strategy. TSCA uses row-level tau switching (one Bernoulli per timestep — harder); SCA uses cell-level lambda mixing (W Bernoulli draws per timestep — easier). TSCA adds step_labels; SCA adds within_var_stats.
comparison_notes.vs_ECANet_New: Different task — TSCA identifies a pair of rules and a switching probability from K orbits; ECANet-New identifies a single rule from a single orbit.
